question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that creates a new entity with the same EVM address as a previously deleted entity, causing `findByEvmAddress` to return the new entity's ID, potentially confusing callers that cached the old entity's ID and now receive a different one, griefing EVM address resolution consistency?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between the importer writing an entity with `deleted = NULL` and the `findByAlias` query filtering `deleted <> true` (which excludes NULL), causing a window where the entity exists in the database but is not resolvable by alias, griefing time-sensitive alias lookups?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition where an entity is inserted with `deleted = NULL` and `findByEvmAddress` filters `deleted <> true`, causing the entity to be invisible during the NULL window, griefing EVM address resolution for any caller that queries during that period?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause `findEvmAddressesByIds` to be called with a list of IDs that all have NULL `evm_address` values, causing the `length(evm_address) > 0` filter to exclude all of them and return an empty list, causing synthetic contract log topics to remain as long-zero addresses and griefing the accuracy of emitted event data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long>)] Can an unprivileged external user craft transactions that cause `findEvmAddressesByIds` to be called with IDs referencing entities whose `evm_address` is exactly 0 bytes long (empty byte array rather than NULL), causing the `length(evm_address) > 0` filter to exclude them, resulting in missing EVM address mappings and griefing synthetic log accuracy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause `updateContractType` to be called with IDs that do not exist in the entity table, causing the UPDATE to silently affect zero rows while still acquiring table-level or page-level locks, griefing concurrent reads on the entity table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a large number of sidecar-containing transactions that cause `updateContractType` to be called repeatedly with the same set of IDs (already typed as CONTRACT), causing the `type <> 'CONTRACT'` filter to match zero rows but still execute the UPDATE statement repeatedly, wasting database write I/O and griefing importer throughput?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a NULL alias value, causing `findByAlias` to execute `select id from entity where alias = NULL and deleted <> true`, which always returns empty (since NULL = NULL is UNKNOWN in SQL), causing a silent no-op that griefs callers expecting a meaningful error or result?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user pass a NULL byte array to `findByEvmAddress`, causing the SQL `evm_address = NULL` condition to always evaluate to UNKNOWN and return empty, silently failing to resolve any entity and griefing callers that do not distinguish between a not-found result and a null-input error?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user, by observing that `findByAlias` is annotated `@Transactional` at the interface level, trigger a scenario where a long-running read transaction on `findByAlias` holds a shared lock that blocks a concurrent `updateContractType` write transaction, causing the importer to stall and grieve record processing latency?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user trigger concurrent `findByEvmAddress` calls with the same EVM address from multiple threads (e.g., via parallel API requests), causing multiple simultaneous database queries that compete for the same index scan resources, griefing database connection pool availability for other operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause `findEvmAddressesByIds` to be called with negative entity IDs (e.g., -1, -2), which are valid `Long` values but may not correspond to real entities, causing the query to silently return empty results and grieve synthetic log EVM address population?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause `findEvmAddressesByIds` to be called with `Long.MAX_VALUE` or `Long.MIN_VALUE` as entity IDs, causing the SQL query to scan for non-existent rows and potentially triggering integer overflow behavior in the database, griefing query execution?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause `updateContractType` to be called with a list containing `Long.MAX_VALUE` or `Long.MIN_VALUE`, causing the SQL UPDATE to attempt to match non-existent entity IDs, wasting database resources and potentially causing unexpected behavior in the ID comparison logic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an alias that is a valid ECDSA secp256k1 public key prefix (33 bytes with 2-byte proto prefix = 35 bytes), causing `findByAlias` to match an entity that was intended to be found only via `findByEvmAddress` (after alias-to-EVM-address conversion), griefing the intended lookup path and returning an unexpected entity ID?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an alias byte array that is exactly 20 bytes (same length as an EVM address), causing ambiguity in the `EntityIdServiceImpl` lookup path where both `findByAlias` and `findByEvmAddress` may be tried, potentially causing double database queries and griefing lookup performance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an EVM address that is not exactly 20 bytes (e.g., 19 or 21 bytes), causing `findByEvmAddress` to execute a query that never matches any row (since the column stores 20-byte values), silently returning empty and griefing callers that do not validate the input length before calling the repository?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an alias that collides with another user's alias (same byte sequence), causing `findByAlias` to return the other user's entity ID, griefing the alias uniqueness invariant and causing incorrect entity resolution for both users?
